narration_text: Now we'll see how multiple internet service providers interact with each other. Look at the diagrams on your slide, notice how they're connected through various nodes and links, illustrating the concept of internet exchange points. These diagrams show how different ISPs can peer with each other, allowing data to be exchanged between their networks. I want you to focus on the complexity of these connections and how they enable global communication. As we can see, the presence of one viable ISP can lead to competition and the establishment of peering links, ultimately forming the backbone of the internet. Let's think about this together, as we move forward, we'll explore how these connections impact the overall structure of the internet.
